name: MCA
mission: To provide religious, social, and educational services to enable the Muslim community to fulfill its responsibilities and contribute to the betterment o...
zakatClaimEvidence: The Muslim Community Association (MCA) explicitly states, "Submit your Zakah request to the Muslim Community Association." and "MCA's core social service is the collection of Zakat and Sadaqah funds to support people in need...". They also have a dedicated "Zakah Request" section on their website under services and mention that "Zakat Al-Fitr will be collected starting March 9th." Furthermore, they have a "Zakah and Relief committee" chartered to facilitate the distribution of Zakah monies. (Source: https://www.mcabayarea.org)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

components.evidence: Accepts zakat (+2); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Asnaf: masakin (+5); Strong humanitarian service (+4)
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.

components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish current reserve levels and a board-approved reserve policy (often 3-12 months for most nonprofits).

score_details.score_summary: Muslim Community Association of San Francisco Bay Area shows good alignment and limited impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: The Muslim Community Association (MCA) explicitly states, "Submit your Zakah request to the Muslim Community Association." and "MCA's core social service is the collection of Zakat and Sadaqah funds to support people in need...". They also have a dedicated "Zakah Request" section on their website under services and mention that "Zakat Al-Fitr will be collected starting March 9th." Furthermore, they have a "Zakah and Relief committee" chartered to facilitate the distribution of Zakah monies. (Source: https://www.mcabayarea.org)

all_citations.claim: The organization's profile metadata and financial fields are tracked by major charity evaluators.
Financial data and organizational structure are reported through standard non-profit filings.
The organization operates programs including social services and a weekend Islamic school.
all_citations.source_name: Donate stock to mca – download file

all_citations.claim: MCA provides religious and educational services to enable the community to fulfill its responsibilities.
The organization is Zakat-eligible and offers various donation options for supporters.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization receives a moderate overall score of 45/100, reflecting a strong alignment with donor needs but a need for more detailed impact reporting and financial transparency.
areas_for_improvement: Limited public availability of recent financial efficiency metrics

dimension_explanations.alignment: The alignment score of 32/50 is supported by the organization's high donor fit for the Muslim community and the religious urgency of its mission [5]. Its long-standing presence in the Bay Area establishes a significant track record for cultural and religious preservation [4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The impact score of 18/50 reflects a direct service model focused on education and social welfare [3]. While the organization provides essential community services, there is a lack of publicly available data regarding specific cost-per-beneficiary metrics or third-party impact evaluations [1][2].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Muslim Community Association of San Francisco Bay Area serves as a vital hub for religious, social, and educational services for the regional Muslim community.
strengths: Designated as Zakat-eligible for religious giving
Offers diverse programming including educational and social services
Provides multiple ways to contribute, including stock donations
baseline_narrative.summary: The Muslim Community Association of San Francisco Bay Area (MCA) provides a comprehensive suite of services including social assistance, Zakah distribution, and a weekend Islamic school [3][4]. As a Zakat-eligible organization, it enables donors to fulfill religious obligations through structured community support programs [5].
